summary: Core reactive MVVM primitives, commands, activation, routing, platform-neutral guidance, and upgrade paths for legacy ReactiveUI applications.
recommendedPatterns: Use ReactiveObject and ReactiveCommand for mutable state and user actions.
Prefer WhenAnyValue and OAPH/source-generator derived properties over imperative setter side effects.
Plan upgrades from legacy ReactiveUI codebases by modernizing startup, disposal, command handling, and generator usage incrementally.
avoidPatterns: Do not create long-lived subscriptions in constructors without disposal.
Do not mutate UI-bound state from background threads.
Do not leave legacy initialization, obsolete collection types, or stale package combinations in upgraded apps when a modern equivalent exists.
setupSteps: Select the correct platform package for the target UI stack.
For legacy upgrades, align packages first, then replace obsolete patterns incrementally with modern ReactiveUI guidance.
commonPitfalls: Mixing imperative state mutation with derived observable state.
Failing to scope bindings with activation on XAML-style platforms during upgrades.

sources.title: ReactiveUI repository
sources.notes: Primary ReactiveUI framework source and upgrade guidance basis.

harvest.sourceSummary: Curated from local ReactiveUI repositories, official GitHub documentation, and current SDK/package guidance.
harvest.notes: Prepared for the ReactiveUI MCP Server knowledge catalog.
